steps.title: Find the grammatical subject and apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is “The trailblazing accomplishments of Goldin.” The head noun is accomplishments — a plural noun. The prepositional phrase “of Goldin” is a modifier and does not change the subject’s number. The blank is the main verb, so it must be plural to agree with accomplishments.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — attests: Third-person singular present. A plural subject requires a plural verb, not a singular one. ✗
  • Choice B — has attested: Present-perfect singular (“has” is a singular auxiliary). Still does not agree with the plural subject accomplishments. ✗
  • Choice C — is attesting: Present-progressive singular (“is” is singular). Same agreement error as A and B. ✗
  • Choice D — attest: Bare plural present-tense verb. Correctly agrees with the plural subject accomplishments. ✓
That is the correct answer.
